body:
## Implementation Plan Complete — BIN-110 Session Handoff & Resume

Plan written for session handoff notes and one-click resume context.

### Summary

- **8 new files**: types/handoff.ts, lib/handoff.ts (localStorage/sessionStorage CRUD), HandoffNoteModal, ResumeContextCard, HandoffHistory with CSS modules
- **4 modified files**: story/[id]/page.tsx + CSS (End session button, resume card, history sidebar), page.tsx + CSS (Quick Resume enrichment)
- `useRef` for debounce timer (never `useState` — BIN-73 lesson applied)
- Home page already `use client` — no conversion needed
- Textarea gets `ref={editorRef}` + `onBlur={handleEditorBlur}` for session tracking
- Tab-scoped snooze via sessionStorage; dismissal via local state (note history never deleted)
- `formatRelativeTime` reused from `@/lib/autosave`

See `<plan>` block in description for exact types, CSS, and acceptance mapping.

Assign to: Code Monkey

## Design brief complete

Design brief for [BIN-110](/BIN/issues/BIN-110) is ready. Updated the issue description with full plan in `<plan>` tags.

**Summary of design decisions:**
- "End session" button in editor header top row (low-friction, secondary styled)
- HandoffNoteModal: fixed bottom-right panel (non-blocking), two optional fields, focus trap, Escape to dismiss
- ResumeContextCard: full-width banner below editor header, shows last scene + note + CTA
- HandoffHistory: replaces sidebar "Notes" placeholder with timestamped note timeline (latest 10)
- Home page Quick Resume: wired to real session data via `handoff-recent-story` localStorage key

**8 new files, 4 modified files.** Full step-by-step implementation plan with types, component specs, CSS classes, exact JSX additions, and acceptance mapping is in the issue description.

Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This stands out by optimizing the highest-friction moment competitors under-serve: the restart between writing sessions. We are not just adding organization; we are creating continuity momentum (intent + next action + exact resume target) that can materially increase manuscript completion rates.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter emphasizes planning/organization (drag-and-drop chapters/scenes, planning/manuscript boards), but does not present a dedicated end-session handoff + resume-context workflow in core docs: https://devlanding.livingwriter.com/
- LivingWriter Version History supports recovery/revert of drafts, which is different from forward-looking next-action continuity on reopen: https://guides.livingwriter.com/version-history
- Campfire emphasizes modular worldbuilding and cross-device drafting, including "pick up where you left off" messaging, but not a structured scene-level handoff note + resume card pattern: https://www.campfirewriting.com/apps and https://www.campfirewriting.com/

## Risks / trade-offs
- Extra prompt at session end can feel intrusive if not lightweight and skippable.
- Cursor restore reliability must be high; misses will reduce trust quickly.
- Resume card density can add UI noise if unresolved queues are not prioritized.

## Recommendation
Approve and send to Design with emphasis on frictionless UX: default-fast handoff capture, clear resume hierarchy (scene > note > next action), and low-noise snooze/dismiss behavior.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
